meta.title: CodeLess — Ship a real product, not just theory
meta.description: A 6-week PM simulation where you run a live product with an AI team and walk out with proof on your CV, not another certificate.

hero.subtitle: Stop collecting theory. Over 6 weeks and 12 sessions you'll run a live product as its PM — while an AI team of stakeholder, designer, developer and QA push back like the real thing — and walk out with proof on your CV, not another PDF.

problem.label: 01 — You won't learn alone
problem.title: Real products aren't built in a lecture hall.
problem.body: They're built in the back-and-forth of a team. From day one you're dropped into a live workspace where the messy, real decisions actually happen.
problem.decision: And every call lands on your desk.

chat.name: Tamar
chat.text: I need the booking flow to feel instant — and can we add reviews too?

chat.name: Natia
chat.text: Mockups attached. Single-screen checkout — less drop-off.

chat.name: Giorgi
chat.text: Which endpoints do we actually need? And is this web or mobile?

chat.name: Ana
chat.text: That story has no acceptance criteria — I can't test it yet.

chat.name: You
chat.text: Noted. Reviews go to the backlog; let's lock scope and ship.

compare.label: 02 — The difference
compare.title: Most courses hand you this.
compare.shredLabel: we shred it. here's what's left:
outcomes: Get feedback from a real team

how.label: 03 — How the course works
how.title: Learn it. Then the team makes you use it.
how.subtitle: Each session you learn a concept — then spend the week applying it hands-on with the team on your live product. Six weeks, two tracks, one portfolio that proves it.

curriculum.label: The full syllabus
curriculum.title: Six weeks. Twelve sessions. One product.
curriculum.subtitle: The teaching sequence is linear and logical; the team's demands are always a little richer than what was just taught. That gap is where the learning happens.

weeks.label: Week 1
weeks.title: Foundations
weeks.learn: What a project and product really are, the core IT roles, and Scrum end to end — pillars, values, events and artifacts.
weeks.team: Tamar introduces the team in character and pitches a raw idea; you dig for the real need. Then she pushes to "just build" while Giorgi demands a full spec — and you settle how the team will actually work.

weeks.label: Week 2
weeks.title: How Products Are Built
weeks.learn: The full SDLC and every role in it, IT architecture, the frontend/backend split, APIs, databases and end-to-end flows.
weeks.team: Each teammate's role clicks into place: Giorgi maps his hand-offs and asks which endpoints and platform you need; Ana explains what she needs to test; Natia waits on a platform call before she designs.

weeks.label: Week 3
weeks.title: Defining & Planning
weeks.learn: Functional vs non-functional requirements, BA techniques, AS-IS / TO-BE, backlog structure, estimation and prioritisation.
weeks.team: A full discovery session where Tamar is vague and changes her mind; then you build the real board while Ana refuses stories with no acceptance criteria and Giorgi disputes your estimates.

weeks.label: Week 4
weeks.title: People & Uncertainty
weeks.learn: The project lifecycle, stakeholder mapping, communication planning, the RACI matrix, saying no with data, and risk management.
weeks.team: Tamar returns with new demands and a tighter deadline — some valid, some scope creep — and you push back with a RACI. Giorgi and Ana surface technical and quality risks nobody planned for.

weeks.label: Week 5
weeks.title: Contracts & Success
weeks.learn: Build-vs-buy and procurement, RFP / SOW / NDA, compliance as a decision factor, plus product vs project metrics, KPIs and OKRs.
weeks.team: Tamar wants a feature shipped fast and floats an external vendor; Giorgi warns of GDPR implications. Then she asks "is this working?" and expects real KPIs, not vanity metrics.

weeks.label: Week 6
weeks.title: Shipping & Capstone
weeks.learn: What a release actually takes, pre-launch readiness, formal closure and hand-over, and how to present the whole idea-to-launch story.
weeks.team: Across launch you weigh Tamar's final objection, Giorgi's tech debt and Ana's last bug, then get formal sign-off. In the capstone the whole team questions your journey in character and you run the retro live.

team.label: 04 — The team relay
team.title: You'll play every part of the handoff.
team.hintMobile: Tap a role to see what they do

roles.name: Analyst
roles.desc: Turns a fuzzy idea into a real spec.

roles.name: Designer
roles.desc: Shapes the screens and how it feels.

roles.name: Developer
roles.desc: Writes the code that makes it run.

roles.name: QA
roles.desc: Breaks it before your users can.

roles.name: You · PM
roles.desc: Call the shots and ship it live.

outcomes.label: 05 — The payoff
outcomes.title: You leave with proof, not paper.
outcomes.subtitle: No certificate to frame. A shipped product, the artifacts behind it, and the story of how you got there.

points.title: A shipped, live product
points.desc: With a URL you can hand to a recruiter, and the commit history to prove you built it.

points.title: 30+ real deliverables
points.desc: One-pager, backlog, RACI, risk register, KPIs, launch sign-off — the artifacts real PMs produce.

points.title: 100% hands on keyboard
points.desc: No passive watching. Every week you make decisions and ship the consequences.

register.label: Apply
register.title: Seats are limited to keep teams real.
register.subtitle: Cohort 04 caps at 25. Drop your email and we'll send the syllabus + an interview slot.

footer.tagline: The PM simulation where you ship a real product. Build proof, not paper.
